mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate compresses the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid one-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending recession risk, which compresses near-term earnings expectations and drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when the trigger activates, or the signal produces a positive Rank IC over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in residential construction starts and consumer credit balances indicates robust housing demand and expanding household balance sheets, supporting positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered observations, or interaction terms show zero statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production directly reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to immediate positive momentum in cyclical equity sectors over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month changes show zero correlation with 5-day SPX returns during early-cycle periods, or the signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating monthly inflation forces market pricing of aggressive future rate hikes, increasing discount rates and depressing equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not significantly negative following inflation spikes above 0.4% MoM, or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in real GDP growth and nonfarm payroll expansion confirms broad-based economic strengthening, driving sustained positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or zero Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant during high-volatility periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm employment reflects underlying economic momentum that directly supports near-term corporate revenue expectations and positive weekly equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes show no predictive power for 5-day SPX direction over a 5-year sample, or the signal produces a Rank IC below 0.02 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sub-900k housing starts indicate severe housing sector distress, which historically precedes broader economic contractions and generates negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not significantly negative when housing starts remain below 900k for two consecutive months, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns during credit crunch episodes.
